title: Fermented Chewy Polish Fruit Mix Dumplings
description: A traditional Polish dish featuring a mix of fruits, slow-cooked to create a chewy and romantic dessert, low in carbs and perfect for special occasions. The fermentation process gives it a unique taste and texture.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the mixed berries, diced apples, and plums.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the almond flour, coconut sugar, eggs, sour cream, vanilla extract, and salt.
Add the dry ingredients to the fruit mixture and stir until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a warm place, covered with a cloth, and let it ferment for 2 hours.
Divide the dough into 8-10 equal pieces and shape each into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk shape and place on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the dumplings are golden brown and chewy.
Remove from the oven and let cool for 10 minutes before serving.
